channel.title: Alex Wilson's writing
channel.description: Alex on engineering, products & everything in-between

item.title: Weeknotes: Veteran Of The Psychic Wars — Week 28, 2026
item.description: This was a good week for eating Okinawan food and for achieving several huge paperwork wins. I’ve unlocked a whole new world of being fiscally responsible. Received a few nice voice notes via my sister who bumped into…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This was a good week for eating Okinawan food and for achieving several huge paperwork wins. I’ve unlocked a whole new world of being fiscally responsible.
  • Received a few nice voice notes via my sister who bumped into my high-school maths teacher. “Maths is a game and we invent the rules”. Words to live by!
  • Marked this year’s tanabata in a symmetrical way, but this time instead of searching for a tree, I shared fish & chips and haggis in Osaki and reflected on everything that happened since this time last year. In some ways I don’t recognise myself, in a good way.
  • Separately, there’s something rather poetic about seeing dishes of the UK’s north and south on one plate.
  • What if Yotsu crew, but does darts? Anyway I’m still quite bad at darts.
  • Converted an old e-reader into a TRMNL and now (thanks to the magic of command strips) my digital signage dreams are realised. My todo list, calendar and even some headlines are now displaying on my wall. I hope this doesn’t awaken anything in…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: The Memory Remains — Week 27, 2026
item.description: This week I’ve been thinking about career paths, about how to quantify and measure things and social capital. Oh and the humidity has been rising fast. Re-established my gym routine, equipped with a new (and much…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• This week I’ve been thinking about career paths, about how to quantify and measure things and social capital. Oh and the humidity has been rising fast.
  • Re-established my gym routine, equipped with a new (and much cheaper/better) app for planning and tracking.
  • Did that thing this week where when people brought problems, instead of just listening, I went full career coach mode. This isn’t a muscle I’ve worked out much lately and I forgot how much I enjoy it. More of this?
  • Bittersweet leaving catch-up during the week. It seems like every 6 months or so there’s a small spike of people leaving, sometimes the company and sometimes the city or country. I wish everyone lots of success.
  • Nice little dinner on Thursday which drifted into the wee hours and all over Tokyo. Shibuya can be fun.
  • Costco on Friday. I have restocked coffee and cheese. The kalbi bake is delicious, OMG.
  • Saturday was July the 4th, so I went to a BBQ which was very fun where I ate way too much meat. One highlight…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Instrumental Medley — Week 26, 2026
item.description: Big week. Got mostly good news. Made some principled decisions. Graduated to a retainer. For my first non-technical post on this blog, I published a short speech in Japanese called “The Same Hobby”. There’s a story…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Big week. Got mostly good news. Made some principled decisions. Graduated to a retainer.
  • For my first non-technical post on this blog, I published a short speech in Japanese called “The Same Hobby”. There’s a story behind the speech, and another behind the column layout (check it out on desktop, and with a screen-reader!), long story short is that I want to do more like this.
  • Leaving drinks at the pop-up terrace were very nice. Even the part where an unexpected visitor dropped onto us from a tree, before scurrying off. It’s nice doing this stuff outdoors.
  • No, don't worry, nobody broke their teeth.
  • By focusing very hard, for the first time ever, I understood the closing words of one of my all-Japanese meetings. I needed a nap afterwards but I am chuffed to no end.
  • Speaking of which, new class started. It is, um, a step up. Let's say that.
  • Friday marked the intersection of several big earthquakes and two typhoons. Reader, I’ll never get used to emergency alerts. So anyway…

Read the full post here...

item.title: The Same Hobby
item.description: As part of a recent language programme, I was asked to give a short speech in Japanese. I'm finding it quite challenging to learn, so this was a golden opportunity to talk about something else it is teaching me. Setup…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
As part of a recent language programme, I was asked to give a short speech in Japanese. I'm finding it quite challenging to learn, so this was a golden opportunity to talk about something else it is teaching me.

Setup:

皆さん、最近、面白いことがわかりました。
私の三つの趣味は、同じ趣味です。

Folks uh, recently I realised something interesting: My three hobbies are the same hobby.

Triple:

その三つは、エレクトロニックな音楽と、走ることと、日本語の勉強です。

The three are: electronic music, running, and studying Japanese.

Beat 1:

エレクトロニックな音楽が好きです。

その音楽は、めちゃ長いです。多分、八分ぐらいです？

でも、大きいところはありません。

ゆっくり変わります。

最後は、ぜんぜん違う音楽になります。

I like electronic music.

Tracks are very long. Probably around 8 minutes?

But there's no big moment.

It changes slowly.

And at the end, it becomes completely different music.

Beat 2:

走ることも、同じです。

「今日、速くなりました」と思いません。

でも、一年後、違う人になります。

Running is the same too.

I don't think, “Today I got faster”.

But after a year, I become a different person.

Beat 3:

日本語も、同じです。

Studying Japanese is the same too.

Payoff:

一番面白いことは、何ですか。

「つまらない」か「私は下手です」と思う時です。

その時が、一番な大切です。

でも、やめません。続けます。

そして、何かが変わります！

The most interesting thing?

It's when you think, "This is boring" or "I'm bad at this".

That's the most important time.

You don't quit. You keep going.

…


Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Dirty Window — Week 25, 2026
item.description: Most of my time this week went into goal-setting and on learning Japanese. On goal-setting: This was both attending training, and writing/reviewing goals for my department. Nearly there. On learning Japanese: I have…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Most of my time this week went into goal-setting and on learning Japanese.
  • On goal-setting: This was both attending training, and writing/reviewing goals for my department. Nearly there.
  • On learning Japanese: I have been trying new tools and routine to see what works well, did orientation for my new classes, and have formally verified out that I am now somewhere between N5 and N4! And unexpectedly I played the role of translator on Friday.
  • It felt like every day this week I got a different bit of good (or big) news from my friends' lives, which really made the week.
  • Voice is mostly back, but I will be a bit more careful for the coming weeks. I have this nagging suspicion that my skincare meds are related!
  • I really like that park we go to sometimes.
  • The new blender arrived and it has a grater attachment. So: I bought a block of gruyère, put the whole thing through the blender and made a croque monsieur. Reader, inexplicably I do not enjoy grating cheese, so this is game-changing.
  • Speaking of blending: I am building a feed reader, so I can build a timeline which balances the signal:noise ratio of the sites I follow. Some I read religiously (i.e. weeknotes), and others I skim (i.e. press releases), but I find it hard to track both. Feed-fetching is based on DLT…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Thorn Within — Week 24, 2026
item.description: To set the scene: Last weekend I lost my voice, and then on Monday I woke up feeling totally ill. Maybe it was change of seasons, maybe it was the typhoon, or, maybe it was just not taking a break since in months…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• To set the scene: Last weekend I lost my voice, and then on Monday I woke up feeling totally ill. Maybe it was change of seasons, maybe it was the typhoon, or, maybe it was just not taking a break since in months. Probably a mixture of all three.
  • Some things I have learned about losing your voice:
    • It wasn’t as big a deal as I felt: Nobody minded postponing, cancelling or facilitating my meetings. In big meetings that couldn’t be skipped, people were more attentive to chat than normal. So I typed. A lot.
    • Communicating without speaking is really hard: Trying to describe my symptoms to my doctor was really hard. I don’t think I realised how much of a crutch I have found speaking.
    • Talking is a big part of who I have become: I felt like I couldn’t really express myself, and I noticed that even my inner monologue was quieter.
  • OK so how do you spend a week where you’re not going out, and you can’t talk to anyone?
    • You pick up the todo item from 2022 to “Have a personal away day”, combine it with another todo item from 2019 to “Do a SWOT” and compile a 30-page printed document pack and book a closed space in a co-working space for Sunday.
    • You go through your inbox, spring-clean your newsletter & marketing subscriptions, and delete over a decade of emails.
    • You research and bid on auctions for a blender so that you can make your own peanut-butter and soups, instead of relying on the comparatively expensive store-bought versions you’ve been on since last year.
    • You update the Life/Travel in UK doc you’ve been sharing with friends/colleagues and add a section about Birmingham, and in doing so realise that Birmingham’s Chinatown is genuinely better than London’s one.
    …

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Only Happy When It Rains — Week 23, 2026
item.description: Lots of small things on the docket this week, which made me think back to Rocks, Pebbles & Sand. I broke down all of my ongoing work and priorities in this way and shared it with my team. Hopefully this will earn me…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Lots of small things on the docket this week, which made me think back to Rocks, Pebbles & Sand. I broke down all of my ongoing work and priorities in this way and shared it with my team. Hopefully this will earn me spinach.
  • One of the pebbles was starting a trial for an internal tool which had been blocked by various approvals for over a month. After waiting over a month, it was very satisfying to see my work, “just work”.
  • This week we had a typhoon, which wasn’t super severe, but it was my first time (I think) actually sitting through one. The air was heavy, and everything was wet.
  • Thursday night a few of us gathered to enjoy souvenirs from Italy and Poland, and this was heaps of fun.
  • Friday night I went to a nomikai and made a fatal mistake, finding myself a couple of megas ahead. I overdid it slightly and had to sit out for a bit, and got home in the wee hours.
  • Met up with some friends on Saturday to go to pride, which was more-or-less the same as last year. Lots of sponsors…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Cure — Week 22, 2026
item.description: Another week, another special assignment. This one particularly interesting as it involves applying AI in a novel way. We do a lot of prep over the week. At the same time, I have started writing objectives for the…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Another week, another special assignment. This one particularly interesting as it involves applying AI in a novel way. We do a lot of prep over the week. At the same time, I have started writing objectives for the next two quarters which is surprisingly hard.
  • Yakiniku on Monday night is very good. This won’t be my last time at GEMs this week.
  • Hey, good news, I no longer have braces! Bad news: I now have a retainer. My dentist is positively chuffed that we got here, almost exactly six months after we started. I'm just glad that it'll be quicker to brush my teeth.
  • The project I’ve been focussed on recently has a nice little nomikai and I have a few equally nice 1:1s. It’s night and day where we are now compared to where we started.
  • I received a gift mid-week: Club Mate!!!!!
  • This week is month-end, which for me now means a decent chunk of paperwork. I’m summoned to a dinner, where I’m a bit negative because I’m in a weird mood for many reasons and trying to convince anyone who’ll…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: The More I See — Week 21, 2026
item.description: Monday I took the day off to do some personal shopping as a follow-up to the image consulting. The hardest part was buying new shoes, because virtually nowhere stocks my size. But it was great and I got a few new ideas…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Monday I took the day off to do some personal shopping as a follow-up to the image consulting. The hardest part was buying new shoes, because virtually nowhere stocks my size. But it was great and I got a few new ideas.
  • Yeah so anyway the shoes have already chewed through one of my heels. Let’s try this again in a few weeks.
  • We’re in evaluation season, and as well as doing my own, for the first time in a long time it’s my turn to hear other people’s evaluations and later to calibrate them. This is interesting because — I realise that — since arriving in Japan I have been guessing what evaluators want to hear based on my experience in the UK & BG, and I wish I had known the differences a year ago.
  • Met up with ROL, in town on holiday, for some okonomiyaki and a natter. We walk around Shinjuku for a bit before noticing a place off to the side of a road and saying, “Huh, that looks interesting”. My first time doing the okonomiyaki dance by hand, and gosh, it’s both more fun and much…

Read the full post here...

item.title: Weeknotes: Turn The Page — Week 20, 2026
item.description: Lots of architecture and design work this week. In the run-up to the end of the quarter there’s also a lot of small things to wrap-up but not enough time. Access & Identity (aka Auth) is a fascinating domain. I was…
item.dc:creator: alex
item.content:encoded:
• Lots of architecture and design work this week. In the run-up to the end of the quarter there’s also a lot of small things to wrap-up but not enough time.
  • Access & Identity (aka Auth) is a fascinating domain.
  • I was summoned for some belated birthday drinks at The Aldgate which is a delightful pub. There I received some confirmation bias, and an interesting fortune reading. We tried to decode a curious social dynamic, met someone from my hometown and by the end of the night I had been thoroughly defeated in chess.
  • You might’ve noticed some design tweaks on this blog: Well, channelling enthusiasm I have made some long-overdue improvements including finishing setting up Storybook so I can do more design work in isolation, and fixing gripes with Gatsby + porting it to TypeScript.
  • The Yotsu Crew met up for some sake, some dinner and finished by taking the train for cocktails at Coto Academy. Mine was absinthe which was bold for a school night.
  • Unfortunately an event I was looking forward…

Read the full post here...
